# Hyperion Delta

> Zero-config local agent state management for dirty-set-scale rollback. Rollback that scales with what your agent changed — 55,000x faster than Git on Linux, 9-150x faster on Windows NTFS. Built for AI coding agents running MCTS-style repair loops.

## Getting Started

- [Quickstart](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/guides/getting-started/): Install hyperion-delta, create a session, run attempts with automatic checkpoint + reconcile + rollback, promote successful attempts, dispose.
- [Core Concepts](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/guides/concepts/): Checkpoint snapshot lifecycle, reconcile firewall for child-process writes, atomic rollback via temp-file rename, promotion without Git, VFS interception of sync/callback/promise/stream APIs.

## Architecture

- [Thesis](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/architecture/thesis/): Why rollback must scale with dirty set, not repository size. 55,000x benchmark evidence from 50K-file workspace audit. Metadata bottleneck lesson from full-tree clone/delete failure. Product boundary: Hyperion is not Git.
- [Strategy Tiers](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/architecture/strategies/): tmpfs RAM-backed (Tier 1, Linux/WSL2), POSIX hard links (Tier 2, macOS/Linux), NTFS hard links (Windows), Hot Dirty Buffer (all platforms, in-memory before disk spill), Pure Manifest (Tier 3, universal baseline). Auto-selection logic and fallback behavior.
- [Safety Model](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/architecture/safety/): Reconcile firewall (mandatory before rollback, catches native-tool writes), atomic restore (temp file + rename), ghost directory cleanup, integrity errors (fail loudly on missing backups), strict ignored-write policy, tool output contracts via declareToolOutputs().
- [Git Companion](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/architecture/git-companion/): Three-layer model (Git owns history, Hyperion owns attempt isolation, agent framework owns planning). Durable attempt journals, patch export via exportPatch(), promotion without Git, recovery rehydration from journal metadata.

## API Reference

- [HyperionWorkspace](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/api/workspace/): Low-level API: snapshot(), reconcile(), rollback(), promote(), dispose(), track(), declareToolOutputs(), getDiagnostics(), recoverAttempts(), rehydrateAttempt(), exportPatch(), installFsInterceptor(), uninstallFsInterceptor(). Constructor accepts string path or full HyperionConfig.
- [Agent Session](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/api/agent-session/): High-level wrapper with autopilot runAttempt(callback, options). exec() helper runs explicit executable + args array with shell:false. Context exec() reconciles after child-process completion. Session-specific errors include HyperionExecError, HyperionExecTimeoutError, HyperionExecOptionsError, HyperionAttemptContextError, and HyperionAttemptRollbackError.
- [Types and Errors](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/api/types/): Complete type reference: HyperionConfig (14 fields with defaults), StorageStrategyKind, ReconcileResult, CheckpointId. Error hierarchy: HyperionError → HyperionCapacityError, HyperionIntegrityError, HyperionPathError → HyperionIgnoredPathError, HyperionRollbackError. Tool contracts, diagnostics, recovery types.

## Guides

- [Limitations](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/guides/limitations/): Four known limitations with decided mitigations. No permanent history/merging (Git companion bridges this). Platform disparity (Hot Dirty Buffer + Windows link discovery). Ignored files blindspot (strict mode + tool contracts). Agent lifecycle complexity (autopilot runner).
- [Reliability](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/guides/reliability/): Reliability SLOs and CI gates. Targets: zero silent corruption, zero rollback invariant violations, <0.1% flake rate, and required linux/windows/macos matrix. Covers failure injection, seeded fuzz, stress smoke, and nightly soak commands.
- [Security](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/guides/security/): Zero runtime dependencies. No arbitrary shell execution. exec() uses spawn with shell:false. Fixed internal probes only (git, rsync, fsutil). Path normalization before I/O. Mandatory reconcile before rollback. Missing backups fail loudly.
- [Troubleshooting](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/guides/troubleshooting/): 14 common issues. Git unavailable (stat-only fallback), tmpfs unavailable (pure manifest degrade), Windows NTFS (hard-link discovery), strict ignored writes (declareToolOutputs), durable journal recovery (recoverAttempts, canRehydrate), rehydration failures (volatile memory-only blockers), patch export (text files only), missing backup records (integrity error).
- [Release and Publishing](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/guides/release/): Local pre-publish gate (`npm run release:final`), GitHub Actions trusted publishing with npm provenance via OIDC (`id-token: write`), tag-based publish flow (`refs/tags/vX.Y.Z`), and manual dispatch fallback from `main`.

## Benchmark Evidence

- [Results](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/benchmark/results/): Original WSL2 audit (55,000x tmpfs, 3,580x manifest). Sweep data: dirty-set scaling (1→100 files: 4→299ms, 153x→2.9x vs Git), repo-size independence (1K→5K: manifest flat at 15ms, Git 3.3x growth to 1,887ms), agent-search stress (5-10 MCTS branches: 45-147ms per cycle).
- [Windows Performance](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/benchmark/windows/): NTFS-only benchmark. 10,000-file repo, 10 dirty files, 62ms average rollback — 9.2x faster than Git. No tmpfs, no Linux, no Git on the hot path. Pure Node.js filesystem operations.
- [Reproduce](https://ayush585.github.io/Hyperion-Delta/benchmark/reproduce/): How to run benchmarks locally. Smoke test (npm run benchmark:smoke), full audit (npm run benchmark), environment overrides (HYPERION_FILE_COUNT, HYPERION_ITERATIONS), WSL2 staging notes, sweep runner usage (node scripts/sweep-runner.mjs config.json).
